francophone.excerpt: Partout dans le monde, les ventes de bracelets, de solitaires et de dormeuses progressent. Une tendance qui n’a pas échappé aux géants du luxe qui veulent mieux exploiter ce segment.

francophone.excerpt: Le Suisse s'est montré "solidaire" du capitaine des Bleus à la suite des posts racistes de la sénatrice paraguayenne Celeste Amarilla publiés sur X, dimanche.

anglophone.title: Zelensky to press Nato for air defence systems after intense Russian strikes
anglophone.excerpt: The Ukrainian president will use the Nato meeting in Turkey to argue he needs more interceptor missiles.

anglophone.title: Charlie Kirk's family attends hearing for suspect charged with his murder
anglophone.excerpt: The hearing will decide whether Tyler Robinson will stand trial in the shooting death of Kirk, a key Trump ally.

anglophone.title: What a divorce coach wishes couples knew before ending a marriage
anglophone.excerpt: Divorce is a tool, not a weapon, says Karen McNenny, author of a new book on the subject. She explains how to end a marriage while protecting your family and your mental health.

anglophone.title: The U.S. men's run at the World Cup ends with a 4-1 Round of 16 loss to Belgium
anglophone.excerpt: The team was in the eye of a storm over a controversial phone call from President Trump to FIFA's head about a red card on a U.S. striker. But even with the U.S. at full strength, Belgium easily won.

anglophone.title: Resistance and revenge - Iran wanted to send a message with its farewell to Khamenei
anglophone.excerpt: Three days of public mourning in Tehran was a political spectacle Iran's leaders wanted the world to see, writes the BBC's international correspondent Lyse Doucet.

anglophone.title: Jailers and officials at Russia's 'torture prisons' in Ukraine exposed by BBC
anglophone.excerpt: Former prisoners accuse the men of abuse in detention centres and want to see them brought to trial.

anglophone.title: Extreme heat on Independence Day will be America's new normal, experts say
anglophone.excerpt: In cities across the U.S., parades were canceled and events were delayed because of the heat. Meanwhile, emergency rooms saw a high number of people with heat-related illnesses.

anglophone.title: This World Cup marks end of era for Ronaldo and other soccer greats

anglophone.excerpt: Ahead of this evening's World Cup match between the U.S. and Belgium, the drama about the eligibility of U.S. striker Folarin Balogun continues to swirl following his red card in the previous match.

anglophone.title: Supreme Court lets Texas restrict minors' access to app stores for the time being
anglophone.excerpt: Texas' App Store Accountability Act requires minors to have their parents' permission to download most apps. The Supreme Court says the law can go into effect as lawsuits continue in lower courts.

anglophone.title: Graham Platner faces growing calls to withdraw following allegation of sexual assault
anglophone.excerpt: The Democratic candidate for Senate in Maine said he was assessing next steps after allegations of sexual assault were reported on Monday by Politico. Platner denied them as "categorically untrue."

anglophone.title: More than 1,000 arrested as part of global human trafficking crackdown
anglophone.excerpt: Interpol says the vast majority of the 2,070 victims or potential victims identified were women being trafficked for sex.

anglophone.excerpt: "Reverend Ray" Broshears founded the queer vigilante group the Lavender Panthers in 1973. The group's impact is still felt today.
